channel.title: Comments for page "Into the Woods"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Into the Woods"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 just for Specialist 0, which was hands down one of my favourite things from the 3k contest. The rest of it is excellent too - this whole setting you've got here is ending up to be pretty disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Annette? As in, Specialist 0? Man, I always hoped she'd appear somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've got to give you credit. You made a conversation that took place half in writing feel incredibly tense. While the previous two entries of this team provided excellent bait, I'd say this is the part of the series that is the real hook.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Completely agree with A Random Day. Also the fact that the Foundation considered putting a projector for the stars on the moon won my upvote.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Memetic shenanigans are hard to pull off but this does so excellently. It bordered on being really confusing but it always pulled itself through to make for a memorable tale.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This series is painting a truly terrifying picture of a Foundation run amok with memes, and I love it so far.

(Also my favorite team name so far)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You really are the best at this kind of writing: it’s almost but not quite at the edge of incomprehensibility and as a result manages to accomplish far more worldbuilding and plot advancement than should be possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh fuck.

I had a feeling that my interpretation of 3475 may have been way off-track, and I didn't really have any idea of what to expect from the next entry. This is a rather chilling picture, though.

So, my wall/Veil analogy from 3475 kind of falls flat a little bit. What I think this is now though is a little harder to describe. It's… like a current. A current of memes in the sea of human thought, that the Foundation used to be able to control pretty effectively. But something really really bad happened, and I don't think I have enough pieces here yet to properly comprehend what it was. I feel like it has to do with the stars disappearing and the huge initiative the Foundation had to take in order to cover that up, but I also feel like there might be more to it than that.

But whatever it was turned this meme current into a maelstrom. The world is ending because the ideas and concepts that make up our world are fucked up, the foundation of our very understanding of what's around us has fallen to pieces, and it's irreparably shattered human thought.

Of course, I may be stupidly wrong again. >.> But I'm as intrigued now as I was with 3475, and inaccurate interpretations of what the fuck is happening be damned, I'm still dying to know what comes next.
